new_attempt.controller_recovery_policy: same-call automatic restart with immutable generation markers, an idempotent claim/submission ledger, a durable cross-app GPU lease, a durable single-job ledger, and no terminalization of controller KeyboardInterrupt

operational_changes.atomic_artifacts: claim, calibration, each shard, discovery aggregate plus selection lock, validation aggregate, atlas, and result are written under immutable UUID generations; an unmarked generation is inert, a named-Dict compare-and-set selects exactly one fully committed generation, and one separately committed hash-inventory marker materializes that selection
operational_changes.claim: a user-supplied or deterministic claim identity is reusable; the same byte-identical launch manifest is accepted across generation, marker, status, and return cuts, while any different manifest fails closed
operational_changes.controller: a durable one-job ledger reattaches an existing Modal call; an interrupted spawn-to-ledger window may enqueue a duplicate that serializes and returns the already-validated artifact
operational_changes.cross_app_gpu_lease: immediately before submission, a named Modal Dict uses atomic put-if-absent on one global slot; the lease has no automatic expiry, same-claim retries resume it, every GPU dispatch verifies it, and completion explicitly releases it
operational_changes.cross_app_scope: all cooperating J-lens Modal launchers must use the named lease; two local app-list checks fail closed on already-active non-cooperating apps, while a separately launched non-cooperating account workload after the final check cannot be atomically controlled by this repository
operational_changes.gpu_dispatch: one Modal GPU function with max_containers=1 covers every GPU phase and jobs run in fixed serial order
operational_changes.keyboard_interrupt: controller KeyboardInterrupt remains nonterminal so Modal same-input preemption restart can resume
operational_changes.result_evidence: the immutable result generation contains byte-pinned controller and submission-state snapshots rather than only hashes of later-mutable root files
operational_changes.source_firewall: the image is assembled file-by-file from an exact allowlist and contains no Git metadata, historical result bundles, confirmatory allocation generators, or source snapshots/manifests from other experiments; a clean local commit plus the complete file-hash inventory is baked into the image and revalidated before every remote phase
operational_changes.shared_lease_adoption: attempt 5 remains ineligible to launch while V6 is active; atomic cross-app exclusion additionally requires every future V6 launcher to acquire the same named-Dict slot before correlation may rely on the shared lease rather than only the two app-list checks
operational_changes.submission: the pre-submit app check, lease receipt, deterministic submission intent, and root call ID are durable; a retry reattaches by call ID or recovers the authoritative controller binding before any safe serialized duplicate
operational_changes.workers: GPU jobs are idempotent and never commit partial work in a finally block

outcome_status_at_freeze: attempt 4 stopped after all eight discovery shard artifact sets but before discovery aggregation, selection lock, or validation; semantic outcome contents were not inspected; attempt 5 not launched and fresh Volume E absent
reason: Attempt 4's CPU controller was preempted and its non-idempotent BaseException path made Modal's automatic same-input restart impossible. This amendment changes only durable orchestration and uses a fresh Volume.
unchanged: all 400 source indices, discovery/validation assignments, shard assignments, rollout seeds, candidates, token families, and literal masks
model, dataset, WikiText, lens, calibration, generation, readout, candidate scoring, selection, permutation, bootstrap, validation, and atlas rules
scanner source and scanner configuration bytes
one global L40S ceiling and no overlapping Modal GPU app
sealed-final, future-reserve, and retired manifests remain unavailable
